prompt: Freelancers waste hours chasing clients for invoices across Gmail, spreadsheets, and apps. Payments are late and it's hard to see who owes what.
Students at a large university struggle to find and book available study rooms across multiple campus buildings. They currently check whiteboards, walk around, or message group chats.
Roommates and friends lose track of shared expenses after trips, dinners, and rent. They use multiple apps and awkward reminders to settle debts.
Elderly patients with chronic conditions forget to take their medications on time. Family members worry but have no visibility into adherence.
People who want to sell second-hand clothes find it tedious to photograph, price, and list items across multiple marketplace apps. Buyers distrust unknown sellers.
Remote teams miss important Slack threads because channels move too fast. Decisions and action items get buried in long conversations.
Online grocery shoppers are frustrated when items are out of stock and replacements are chosen without their input, leading to wrong orders and refunds.
Small e-commerce sellers struggle to give customers accurate delivery updates because they ship through multiple carriers with different tracking systems.
Friends planning a group trip or party spend hours debating dates, locations, and budgets across scattered group chats and spreadsheets.
Junior developers submit pull requests with inconsistent code style, missing tests, and unclear commit messages. Senior engineers spend too much time on repetitive review feedback.
